_metadata.for_match: Houston Dynamo FC vs Louisville City FC
_metadata.competition: 2026 Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cup — Round of 16
_metadata.previous_match_data_at: 2026-04-25 FT: Austin FC 2-0 Houston Dynamo FC (Copa Tejas R1, Nelson 13', Uzuni 47')
_metadata.notes: Louisville is USL — open cup guide framing. Houston storylines lead, opponent is the discovery angle. Louisville beat Austin 2-1 in R32 (April 14, Sean Totsch 26', Tola Showunmi 32'). Houston hosted El Paso in R32 (4-1 win, April 15). LINGR IS BACK — suspension served vs Austin, available for Open Cup. Tola Showunmi has 4 Cup goals and leads Cup Golden Boot — he is the main threat. Taylor Davila is USL Player of the Year 2025. Simon Bird is the interim head coach (took over from Danny Cruz March 2026 — UofL alum, hometown connection). CRITICAL INTEL (Apr 27): Louisville RESTED Showunmi, Totsch, AND Davila for the April 25 USL Cup match vs Fort Wayne — they are sending the A-team to Shell Energy. This is not a casual cup trip. Bird quote on Houston: 'Houston are up there.' First-ever competitive meeting between the clubs. HANDLES VERIFIED: Showunmi=@tolaashow, Davila=@taylor_davila7, Totsch=@tophcity (bio: 'Defender for Louisville City FC, 3-time USL Cup Champion'). McGlynn: sub vs Austin at 55', no new injury — day-to-day. Research cache fresh from April 25, good through May 2. Do NOT run --fresh unless cache check fails.

matchday_hook: After Saturday's Copa Tejas gut punch, the Orange is back at Shell Energy on Wednesday for Open Cup business. Louisville City FC — defending USL Players' Shield winners — beat an MLS team to get here (Austin FC, 2-1 in R32). They're serious. But so is Houston. Ben Olsen has won this cup three times. Ondřej Lingr is BACK from his suspension. And nobody in this city has forgotten 2023. The Open Cup is Houston's trophy. Let's go get it. 🏆🧡
vibe_strip_items: 🏆 OPEN CUP R16 — Houston defending the legacy. Ben Olsen has won this cup as player (DC United 2008), coach (DC United 2013), and Houston coach (2023). He is the cup.
✅ Copa Tejas was rough: Austin 2-0 Saturday. But Open Cup is a new competition. Fresh slate. Home crowd. Shell Energy.
🔥 LINGR IS BACK — suspension served vs Austin. The bicycle kick hero returns for the Open Cup. 78-second goals don't just happen once.
🏆 Louisville beat Austin FC 2-1 in R32 (April 14). Tola Showunmi scored. They are NOT a pushover — they're the defending USL regular-season champions.
👀 Tola Showunmi: 4 Open Cup goals in 2026, leading the Cup Golden Boot race. He's the one to watch.
⭐ Taylor Davila: USL Championship Player of the Year 2025. 25 years old, from Sherman Oaks. Signed through 2028. This is the best player in the second division walking into our building.
🏟️ HOME ADVANTAGE. Shell Energy. 7 PM CT. Paramount+. This is Houston's tournament.

⚠️ Louisville rested Showunmi, Davila, AND Totsch for their April 25 USL match. They saved their whole attack for us. Simon Bird on Houston: 'We need to continue to strive to play against the best teams, and Houston are up there.' They are NOT underestimating this.
🧡 Ibrahim Aliyu's first goal came at the perfect time vs San Diego. Will he or Ponce step up again with the Open Cup on the line?
🎂 Héctor Herrera: scored on his 36th birthday in Orlando. Club legend having himself a return season.

hot_posts.post_date: April 2026 — check for post-Austin road trip content + any reaction to return
hot_posts.description: Czech forward who went VIRAL with a bicycle kick in 78 seconds vs El Paso (MLS Energy Moment of the Matchday). Red card late vs San Diego meant he served suspension at Austin. He's BACK for Open Cup. The man who started the winning run returns for the biggest home game of the spring.
hot_posts.why_it_works: The viral moment player returning from suspension for a cup game is a perfect content arc.
hot_posts.content_angle: He started this entire run with one goal in 78 seconds. He had to watch from home in Austin. Now he's back — and this is Cup time. 🚲🏆

hot_posts.post_date: April 2026 — check for post-Austin content or any training posts
hot_posts.description: Brazilian winger (Augusto on lineup sheets). 9 goal contributions (5G/4A) through 7 MLS matches — first Dynamo player to 9 contributions in first 6 MLS matches since Brian Ching in 2006. Neymar publicly praised him. Turned 31 April 13. The star power of the team.
hot_posts.why_it_works: 20-year club record. Neymar mention. Brazilian flair in Houston.
hot_posts.content_angle: Frame as star power, not team creator. The 20-year record is the stat. The Neymar DM is the gossip. Individual brilliance.

hot_posts.description: Baby girl due July 2026. The countdown is on. Underappreciated workhorse — makes great runs, creates space, doesn't get the glory. Bogusz is the guy doing the work that lets Guilherme shine.
hot_posts.why_it_works: First-time dad energy is universally relatable. Every game is one closer to July.
hot_posts.content_angle: The workhorse who runs for two. Baby due in July — this cup run is his gift to his daughter. Keep it warm and wholesome.

hot_posts.description: Started and scored the Orlando match-winner on his 36th birthday. Club legend returning for 2026. Mexican international, former Porto captain, former Atlético Madrid. Artur quote: 'He and Hector are great friends on and off the field. Happy to play with him again. He turns 36 tomorrow but he still plays at a high level.'
hot_posts.why_it_works: 36 years old, scoring game-winners on the road, in a cup run. The full legend arc.
hot_posts.content_angle: 36 and doing it. Not a legacy signing — STARTED and won the game. Open Cup is his arena too.

hot_posts.description: Lawrence posted a pitch-side photo with Preslee right after the San Diego 1-0 win. Caption: 'Lawrence Ennali Hallelujah!!! 🧡' — celebratory, authentic, posted by the player himself. Fresh Inner Circle content.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Posted by the player himself. Passes the repost test because he posted it.
hot_posts.content_angle: Introduce Preslee Clark as who she IS — luxury makeup artist. Then the pitch-side win photo as the vibe the guide is built on. DO NOT lead with 'his girlfriend.'

hot_posts.description: 25-year-old forward. Returned from Crawley Town FC (England) for a second stint at Louisville City FC. Has scored 4 goals in the 2026 Open Cup — leading the Cup Golden Boot race. Scored vs Austin in R32 (32'). WAS UNUSED SUB vs Fort Wayne April 25 (USL Cup) — Louisville rested him specifically for Shell Energy. He's coming in fresh.
hot_posts.why_it_works: English-trained player coming back for a second stint and scoring for fun. Golden Boot leader is a newsworthy stat even in USL.
hot_posts.content_angle: Discovery framing: 'Who IS this guy?' Came back from England. Now he's hunting a Cup Golden Boot against MLS clubs. If you haven't heard of Tola Showunmi, you're about to.

hot_posts.handle: @taylor_davila7 (VERIFIED — LouCity official Instagram has tagged him in reels)
hot_posts.description: USL Championship Player of the Year 2025. 25 years old, from Sherman Oaks, California. 6'1" midfielder. Signed through 2028 — the centerpiece of Louisville's future. The best player in the second division by award. NOT in the Fort Wayne lineup April 25 — rested for Houston.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Award-winner walking into Shell Energy. The talent pedigree is real. Discovery angle.
hot_posts.content_angle: USL Player of the Year wants to prove he belongs against MLS. Young California kid, not yet in the top flight, using the Open Cup as his audition. Does he have what it takes?

hot_posts.platform: N/A — coach reference for Inner Circle/matchday context
hot_posts.description: Interim head coach. University of Louisville alum — played there 2001-2004, named Conference USA Player of the Year 2003. English, from Lincoln. Appointed interim when Danny Cruz departed March 2026. On facing Houston: 'It's great for the club. It's great for our players. We need to continue to strive to play against the best teams, and Houston are up there.' Record as interim: 7-1-1. He's not a caretaker — he's running this.
hot_posts.why_it_works: Coaching his childhood team in a cup run against an MLS side. Emotional underdog narrative.
hot_posts.content_angle: He grew up loving this club before he coached it. Now he's walking into Shell Energy trying to upset the defending champions. The personal stakes make Louisville's story real.

injury_flags.status: AVAILABLE — suspension was served vs Austin FC (April 25). Back for Open Cup.
injury_flags.impact: Major positive — the bicycle kick hero returns. He should be in the starting XI. Redemption arc from Austin.

injury_flags.status: AVAILABLE but building fitness — came on as sub at 55' vs Austin (played ~35 min). No new injury reported. Day-to-day, may not start.
injury_flags.impact: Midfield engine. Likely off the bench rather than starting. Full match availability uncertain.

open_cup_context.competition: 2026 Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cup — Round of 16
open_cup_context.winner_earns: Berth in the 2027 Concacaf Champions Cup
open_cup_context.houston_path: R32: Houston 4-1 El Paso Locomotive FC (April 15) | R16: vs Louisville City FC (April 29)
open_cup_context.louisville_path: R32: Louisville 2-1 Austin FC (April 14, Totsch 26', Showunmi 32')
open_cup_context.ben_olsen_legacy: Won Open Cup as DC United player (2008), DC United head coach (2013), Houston Dynamo head coach (2023). Third coach ever to win Open Cup with two different clubs. Defending it is personal.
open_cup_context.hook: Houston is the defending champion. Olsen built this. Louisville beat an MLS team to be here — they're not walking in as tourists. But Shell Energy in April with Lingr back and the whole squad rested from a Wednesday? This is Houston's home. Defend it. 🏆
